parts.title: Architecture, Authority, Runtime, Directory, and Output Format Contracts
parts.objective: Define foundational Harness boundaries that every later specification and implementation workpack inherits.

runtime_split.python: Validate directory boundaries, JSON versus Markdown formats, and runtime declarations.
runtime_split.codex: Draft natural-language contracts and explain cross-runtime ownership.

acceptance_focus: Every Stage declares primary runtime, Python duties, Codex duties, Validator, and Human Gate.
Markdown remains human semantic authority and JSON remains operational index authority.
No generated contract allows semantic YAML.

parts.title: Stage and Artifact Contracts
parts.objective: Define DRD-00 through DRD-06 contracts, artifact lineage, source permanence, and ordered dependency checks.

runtime_split.python: Snapshot source PRD, calculate hashes, validate artifact manifests, and enforce ordered dependencies.
runtime_split.codex: Generate stage-specific semantic Candidate text from source and approved upstream artifacts.
runtime_split.human_gate: Approve stage outputs when Review A, Review B, or Final Review is required.

acceptance_focus: Every semantic stage re-reads immutable Source PRD.
Every downstream artifact binds approved upstream hashes.
DRD-05 consumes only approved sections and control indexes.

parts.title: Reasoning and PRD Element Adoption Rules
parts.objective: Define inference classes, deductive proof records, PRD element validation, element derivation, and product expansion gap routing.

runtime_split.python: Validate inference record fields, source citations, adoption outcomes, duplicate IDs, and gap routing.
runtime_split.codex: Classify PRD facts, write audit-friendly inference records, and produce Candidate element adoption decisions.
runtime_split.human_gate: Decide inductive candidates and unresolved product expansion questions.

acceptance_focus: Inductive candidates do not enter canonical artifacts without approval.
PRD explicit elements are validated before adoption, normalization, question routing, or rejection.
Input obligations create actionable input, selection, import, or acquisition paths.

parts.title: Interaction Closure Contract
parts.objective: Define node, edge, clickable inventory, Reaction taxonomy, async, handoff, exit, failure, and terminal validation rules.

runtime_split.python: Build and validate graph reachability, node identity, edge targets, overlay closure, legal cycles, and terminal conditions.
runtime_split.codex: Derive tasks, pages, states, overlays, handoffs, clickable elements, and Reaction Candidate descriptions.
runtime_split.human_gate: Review task, page, state, overlay, handoff, and major interaction semantics at Review A.

acceptance_focus: Every clickable element has a Reaction, target, success behavior, failure behavior, and cancellation behavior when applicable.
Every non-terminal node has continuation, recovery, return, or exit.
Failure states explain reasons and provide recovery or exit.

parts.title: Shared Component, Information Presentation, Natural-language Layout, and Figma Compatibility Contracts
parts.objective: Define presentation consistency, shared pattern discipline, layout completeness, content growth behavior, and Figma reconstruction compatibility.

runtime_split.python: Validate registry uniqueness, presentation mode consistency, layout completeness fields, and Figma reconstruction metadata presence.
runtime_split.codex: Draft shared component rationale, presentation mode rationale, layout descriptions, and Figma-compatible reconstruction notes.
runtime_split.human_gate: Approve page elements, shared patterns, presentation modes, and layouts at Review B.

acceptance_focus: Information that requires sustained user processing is not represented only by short-lived feedback.
Shared components compare semantic role, data structure, operation set, state model, information hierarchy, and surface constraints.
Natural-language layout describes hierarchy, containment, ordering, sizing, scrolling, state changes, overlays, and Figma reconstruction.

parts.title: Validation, Review, Promotion, Locks, and Invalidation
parts.objective: Define candidate-only enforcement, validator independence, review and promotion state, lock formats, hash binding, and downstream invalidation.

runtime_split.python: Validate Candidate outputs, preserve validator independence, produce lock hashes, and mark downstream dependencies invalidated when upstream hashes change.
runtime_split.codex: Generate Candidate specs and scoped repair candidates without approval or promotion authority.
runtime_split.human_gate: Approve review decisions, promotion decisions, lock readiness, and semantic repair acceptance.

acceptance_focus: Codex output remains Candidate until Python promotion and Human Gate approval.
Validators are independent from the implementation workpack being checked.
Upstream hash changes invalidate dependent artifacts, skills, workpacks, tests, and locks.

parts.title: Deterministic Compiler Conservation
parts.objective: Define DRD-05 deterministic compilation inputs, output assembly, conservation checks, and read-only post-compile QA boundaries.

runtime_split.python: Deterministically assemble approved sections, tables of contents, references, hashes, and final artifacts without creating new semantic decisions.
runtime_split.codex: Perform read-only consistency QA after compilation when requested by a governed workpack.
runtime_split.human_gate: Approve final compiled document readiness and unresolved issue handling.

acceptance_focus: DRD-05 consumes only approved sections and control indexes.
Compilation does not add pages, states, CTAs, components, interactions, or layout decisions.
Compiler conservation validation detects semantic additions, omissions, and source hash drift.

parts.title: Skills, Workpack Generation, and Spec-to-code Traceability
parts.objective: Define spec-before-code enforcement, skill version binding, implementation workpack generation, and traceability from clauses to code, validators, tests, and commands.

runtime_split.python: Validate SPEC_LOCK references, workpack read/write scopes, code target maps, test obligation matrices, and skill manifest version hashes.
runtime_split.codex: Generate implementation workpack candidates, domain skill candidates, and scoped implementation code only after applicable specs are locked.
runtime_split.human_gate: Approve business skill introduction, workpack scope disputes, and traceability exceptions.

acceptance_focus: Business code is not implemented until applicable Contract, Rule, Projection, and Validator Spec are locked.
Every implementation obligation maps Contract Clause to Rule, Projection, Code Target, Validator, Test, and Acceptance Command.
Skills reference locked specs and never become a second authority.
